description_long: This is a vintage gramophone-style record player featuring a large, flared brass horn for sound amplification, mounted on a decorative wooden base with ornate metallic accents. The base is rectangular and dark-stained, with intricate silver or white inlays on its corners and sides, giving it an antique appearance. A circular turntable platter sits atop the base, holding a black vinyl record with a purple label at its center. The tonearm, made of metal, is curved and positioned over the record, with a visible crank handle on the side for manual operation. The horn is attached to a metal bracket and extends upward and outward, dominating the upper profile of the device. The overall construction suggests a blend of wood, metal, and possibly some plastic or rubber components for the record and stylus assembly.
description: A vintage gramophone record player with a large brass horn, ornate wooden base, and visible turntable platter holding a vinyl record. Features include a curved metal tonearm and a side crank handle.
description_view_0: The record player is seen from a three-quarter angle, highlighting the brass horn, ornate wooden base, and the turntable platter with a purple-labeled record.
description_view_1: A direct front view shows the wide opening of the brass horn, the decorative front panel of the wooden base, and the record centered on the platter.
description_view_2: The side view emphasizes the length and curve of the horn, the crank handle on the right, and the profile of the tonearm over the record.
description_view_3: The rear view centers on the back of the horn and the symmetrical design of the base, with the crank handle visible on the left side.
attribute_turntable_platter: The turntable platter is present, circular, and occupies the central top area of the base. It appears to be made of black plastic or metal, with a visible vinyl record on top featuring a purple label. The platter is smooth, slightly raised, and has subtle grooves indicating its function.
attribute_tonearm: The tonearm is present, metallic, and curved, extending from the right side of the base over the platter. It is medium in length, with a joint near its base for pivoting, and appears to be made of steel or similar metal with a dark finish. The tonearm is positioned over the record, suggesting it is ready for playback.
attribute_stylus: The stylus is not clearly visible, but its presence is implied at the end of the tonearm where it meets the record.
